arts.ttl: “One of the most promising” | West Ham eye one of the Premier League’s best young strikers
arts.sum: A young, highly rated, Premier League striker could be on his way to West Ham. Signing a No.9 who’s able to consistently score in claret and blue is something West Ham have struggled with for years. Both Gianluca Scamacca and Sebastian Haller were never able to prove themselves in England, while Danny Ings’ improved form... arts.ttl: Howe: We were the dominant team at half-time
arts.sum: Despite the positives, Howe was left to rue the individual errors from his players in defence as Newcastle missed the chance to move into the top six, with his side now in 10th place. The Magpies head coach believed that Newcastle were the better team in the first half and said the final score would have been different had they converted one of their chances in that period.

arts.ttl: Bowen: The group we have is so good and there is a real togetherness
arts.sum: Jarrod Bowen praised the "togetherness" within West Ham United despite a tough start to the season as he reflected on the Hammers' impressive 2-0 victory over Newcastle United . It's down to me to show the new players what it means to play for this club," Bowen said to Sky Sports after the match.

arts.ttl: “Remain interested” | Striker once again linked with a move to West Ham
arts.sum: West Ham have once again been linked with a move for their long term striker target. Whether it’s journalists making assumptions or the club genuinely being obsessed, West Ham can not stop being linked with signing Youssef En-Nesyri. arts.ttl: “Unbelievable display” | West Ham’s shock win at Newcastle could change their season
arts.sum: A surprising but well deserved win at Newcastle gives West Ham a new lease of life. Heading into the clash in the North East, everyone, including their own fans, had written West Ham off. While Newcastle were in brilliant form, the Irons hadn’t put in a solid performance in any of their last four matches.... arts.ttl: Howe 'disappointed' with 'disjointed' end to Newcastle defeat
arts.sum: Newcastle United's Eddie Howe says he is "disappointed" with his side's "disjointed" performance in the final 30 minutes after the Magpies lost 2-0 against West Ham at St James' Park.
